####################
THE RULE:
FCLASS.D classifies a double‑precision floating‑point value using the same classification categories as the single‑precision floating‑point classify instruction.

THE BACKGROUND INFORMATION:
##########
INSTRUCTION: FCLASS.D
FORMAT: fclass.d rd, fs1
##########
####################

Note:
+ <result> must be a single register condition.
+ <result> must be in this format: <register> <operator> <value>.
+ <register> should be general-purpose registers (it can be floating-point registers also, or vector registers).
+ <operator> can either be '=' or '!='.
+ <value> can either be hexadecimal or decimal.
+ There can be multiple <result> (for example, # Result: sstatus != 0xffffffff & sstatus != 0xffffffffffffffff) (another example, # Result: a0 = 0 | a1 = 0).
+ The initial values of all general-purpose registers, or floating, or vector registers, are 0.
+ Since the initial values of many CSR fields are unknown and may contain implementation-defined bits, generate test cases that isolate the target field using a bitwise AND mask on the CSR readback value instead of checking the full CSR value directly.
+ <extension> must be NONE if the rule doesn't explicitly say about any extension required.
+ The kernel always starts in M-mode.
+ If you are from M-mode and need to go to S-mode or HS-mode, do not use MRET instruction; instead, use this assembly code -> 'call machine_to_supervisor'. This function already has MRET instruction. Do not make a label or branch called "machine_to_supervisor:".
+ If you are from M-mode and need to go to U-mode, do not use MRET instruction; instead, use this assembly code -> 'call machine_to_user'. This function already has MRET instruction. Do not make a label or branch called "machine_to_user:".
+ If you are from M-mode and need to go to VS-mode, do not USE MRET instruction; instead, use this assembly code -> 'call machine_to_virtual'. Do not make a label or branch called "machine_to_virtual:".
+ DO NOT USE '.globl' and '_start'; assume all boilerplate is already set up!
+ Do not use assembler directives or pseudo-instruction encodings such as .inst, .word, .dword, .byte, .half, .quad, or relocation expressions like %pcrel_hi, %pcrel_lo, %hi, %lo; use only standard RISC-V assembly instructions and registers.
+ The kernel info: XLEN = 64; RISC-V 64-bit (RV64); PC initial value is 2147484134.
+ If a test case triggers a trap, execution stops at the trap. Basically, any other instructions should be performed before the trapping instruction.
+ Read THE BACKGROUND INFORMATION.
============================================================
=========
RESPONSE:
=========
============================================================
# Extension Must Be Active: D
    li t0, 0x7ff8000000000000
    fmv.d.x ft0, t0
    fclass.d a0, ft0
# Result: a0 = 0x200

    li      t0, 1
    sw      t0, 0(sp)

    sw      t0, 8(sp)
    li      t0, -4
    sw      t0, 12(sp)
    li      t0, 5
    sw      t0, 16(sp)
    li      t0, 6
    sw      t0, 20(sp)
    li      t0, -7
    sw      t0, 24(sp)
    li      t0, 8
    sw      t0, 28(sp)
    li      a0, 4
    vsetvli t0, a0, e32,m1
    vle32.v v1, (sp)
    vle32.v v2, 16(sp)
    vaadd.vv v3, v2, v1
    vmv.x.s a1, v3
    addi    sp, sp, 32
# Result: a1 = 6

RESPONSE:
=========

# Extension Must Be Active: V
    li a0, 1
    vsetvli t0, a0, e64, m1
    li a1, -16
    vmv.v.x v0, a1          # vs1 = -16
    li a2, 4
    vmv.v.x v1, a2          # vs2 = 4
    vaadd.vv v2, v1, v0     # ( -16 + 4 ) >> 1 = -6
    vmv.x.s a3, v2          # move result to a3
# Result: a3 = -6

vsetvli t0, zero, e64, m1

THE BACKGROUND INFORMATION:
##########

Note:
+ <result> must be a single register condition.

FORMAT: and rd, rs1, rs2
##########
####################

Note:
+ <result> must be a single register condition.
+ <result> must be in this format: <register> <operator> <value>.
+ <register> should be general-purpose registers (it can be floating-point registers also, or vector registers).
+ <operator> can either be '=' or '!='.
+ <value> can either be hexadecimal or decimal.
+ There can be multiple <result> (for example, # Result: sstatus != 0xffffffff & sstatus != 0xffffffffffffffff) (another example, # Result: a0 = 0 | a1 = 0).

=========

# Extension Must Be Active: V
    # Clear fflags
    csrw    fflags, x0

    # Prepare two double‑precision values in memory on the stack
    #   0x4010000000000000  = 4.0  (safe)
    #   0xC010000000000000  = -4.0 (will cause invalid operation on sqrt)
    li      t0, 0x4010000000000000      # 4.0
    li      t1, 0xC010000000000000      # -4.0
    sd      t0, 0(sp)                    # store at sp+0
    sd      t1, 8(sp)                    # store at sp+8

    # Set VL = 1 element, SEW = 64, LMUL = 1  (only the first element is active)
    li      a0, 1
    vsetvli t1, a0, e64,m1

    # Load the two elements into vector register v2 (both will be present,
    # but only the first element is active because VL=1)
    vlse.v  v2, (sp), 8                 # stride 8 bytes (load both doubles)

    # Perform sqrt on the vector; active element (4.0) is fine,
    # inactive element (-4.0) would raise an invalid‑operation exception
    vsqrt.v v3, v2

    # Read back fflags; mask off all bits except the NV (bit 4) to isolate it
    csrr    t2, fflags
    andi    t2, t2, 0b00010000          # keep only NV flag

# Result: t2 = 0
======================================================= END - 60 =======================================================

# Extension Must Be Active: NONE
li t0, 0x80                # MTIE = 1<<7
csrs mie, t0
li t0, 0x80                # delegate timer interrupt to S-mode (bit 7 in mideleg)
csrs mideleg, t0
la t0, trap_handler
csrw stvec, t0
call machine_to_supervisor

# ---- Supervisor mode ----
li t0, 0x20                # STIE = 1<<5
csrs sie, t0

csrs sstatus, t0
wfi                       # wait for supervisor timer interrupt

csrr t1, mcause

and a0, t1, t2            # a0 = mcause & mask
# end of test
# Result: a0 = 0x8000000000000005

+ Do not use assembler directives or pseudo-instruction encodings such as .inst, .word, .dword, .byte, .half, .quad, or relocation expressions like %pcrel_hi, %pcrel_lo, %hi, %lo; use only standard RISC-V assembly instructions and registers.

# Extension Must Be Active: NONE
    la t0, timer_trap
    csrw mtvec, t0
    li t1, 0x80                # MTIE bit in mie
    csrs mie, t1
    li t2, 0x8                 # MIE bit in mstatus
    csrs mstatus, t2
    wfi

timer_trap:
    csrr t3, mcause            # read mcause
    li t4, 0x8000000000000007 # mask for INT bit and code 7
    and t5, t3, t4
    li t6, 0x8000000000000007 # expected value
    xor t7, t5, t6
    sltiu a0, t7, 1            # a0 = 1 if mcause == expected, else 0
# Result: a0 = 1

THE BACKGROUND INFORMATION:
##########
REGISTER: mcause
CSR ADDRESS: 0x342
FIELDS: 63..63=INT, 62..0=CODE
##########
####################

Note:
+ <result> must be a single register condition.
+ <result> must be in this format: <register> <operator> <value>.
+ <register> should be general-purpose registers (it can be floating-point registers also, or vector registers).
+ <operator> can either be '=' or '!='.
+ <value> can either be hexadecimal or decimal.
+ There can be multiple <result> (for example, # Result: sstatus != 0xffffffff & sstatus != 0xffffffffffffffff) (another example, # Result: a0 = 0 | a1 = 0).
+ The initial values of all general-purpose registers, or floating, or vector registers, are 0.
+ Since the initial values of many CSR fields are unknown and may contain implementation-defined bits, generate test cases that isolate the target field using a bitwise AND mask on the CSR readback value instead of checking the full CSR value directly.
+ <extension> must be NONE if the rule doesn't explicitly say about any extension required.
+ The kernel always starts in M-mode.
+ If you are from M-mode and need to go to S-mode or HS-mode, do not use MRET instruction; instead, use this assembly code -> 'call machine_to_supervisor'. This function already has MRET instruction. Do not make a label or branch called "machine_to_supervisor:".
+ If you are from M-mode and need to go to U-mode, do not use MRET instruction; instead, use this assembly code -> 'call machine_to_user'. This function already has MRET instruction. Do not make a label or branch called "machine_to_user:".
+ If you are from M-mode and need to go to VS-mode, do not USE MRET instruction; instead, use this assembly code -> 'call machine_to_virtual'. Do not make a label or branch called "machine_to_virtual:".
+ DO NOT USE '.globl' and '_start'; assume all boilerplate is already set up!
+ Do not use assembler directives or pseudo-instruction encodings such as .inst, .word, .dword, .byte, .half, .quad, or relocation expressions like %pcrel_hi, %pcrel_lo, %hi, %lo; use only standard RISC-V assembly instructions and registers.

+ If a test case triggers a trap, execution stops at the trap. Basically, any other instructions should be performed before the trapping instruction.
+ Read THE BACKGROUND INFORMATION.
============================================================
=========
RESPONSE:
=========

# Extension Must Be Active: NONE
call machine_to_supervisor
li t0, 0x200                # delegate external interrupt (code 9) to S-mode
csrw mideleg, t0
la t1, trap_handler
csrw stvec, t1
li t0, 0x200                # enable external interrupt in S-mode
csrs sie, t0
li t0, 0x2                  # set SSTATUS.SIE = 1 (global interrupt enable)
csrs sstatus, t0
li t0, 0x200                # set pending supervisor external interrupt
csrw sip, t0
wfi                         # wait for interrupt
j done                      # should not be reached

trap_handler:
    csrr a0, mcause         # capture mcause
    li t0, 0x800000000000000F
    and a0, a0, t0          # mask to INT bit and low 4 bits
    j trap_end

done:
    j done

trap_end:
    j trap_end

# Result: a0 = 0x8000000000000009

done:

+ <result> must be in this format: <register> <operator> <value>.

# Extension Must Be Active: NONE
    #-----------------------------------------------------------------
    # Install a simple trap handler that saves mcause into t0 and returns
    #-----------------------------------------------------------------
    # Set mtvec to point to trap_handler (direct mode)
    auipc   t0, 0          # t0 = PC + high‑20 bits of handler address
    addi    t0, t0, trap_handler - .
    csrw    mtvec, t0

    #-----------------------------------------------------------------
    # Trigger a store page‑fault exception
    #-----------------------------------------------------------------
    li      t1, -1                 # address 0xFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFF (likely unmapped)
    sd      x0, 0(t1)              # store to cause page‑fault

    #-----------------------------------------------------------------
    # Execution resumes here after mret from trap_handler
    # Extract INT field (bit 63) and exception code (bits 0‑11)
    #-----------------------------------------------------------------
    srli    t1, t0, 63            # t1 = INT bit
    andi    t2, t0, 0xFFF         # t2 = exception code (low 12 bits)

# Result: t1 = 0 & t2 = 15

    #-----------------------------------------------------------------
    # Trap handler (placed after main flow)
    #-----------------------------------------------------------------
trap_handler:
    csrr    t0, mcause           # save mcause into t0
    mret                           # return from exception

RESPONSE:
=========
============================================================
# Extension Must Be Active: NONE
    la t0, trap_entry
    csrw mtvec, t0          # set trap vector
    li t1, 0                # flag to distinguish trap entries
    csrrw x0, 0xFFF, x0    # first illegal instruction -> first trap
    j .                     # should never reach here

trap_entry:
    beqz t1, first_trap
second_trap:
    csrr a0, mcause         # read mcause on double‑trap entry
    andi a0, a0, 0x7FFFFFFFFFFFFFFF   # mask out interrupt bit
    j .                     # stop here, a0 should be 0x10

first_trap:
    li t1, 1                # mark that we are in first trap handler
    csrrw x0, 0xFFF, x0    # second illegal instruction -> double trap
    j .                     # should never return

# Result: a0 = 0x10

    li     t0, 1 << 4         # bit 4 = LAM (load address misaligned)
    or     t1, t1, t0
    csrw   0x302, t1          # write medeleg with LAM delegated

    call   machine_to_supervisor   # switch to S-mode

    li     t0, trap_handler
    csrw   stvec, t0                # set supervisor trap vector

    li     a1, 0x1003               # misaligned address
    ld     a2, 0(a1)                # cause load address misaligned trap

    # continue after trap
    nop

trap_handler:
    li     a0, 0xdeadbeefdeadbeef   # indicate trap was delegated to S-mode
    sret

# Extension Must Be Active: NONE
    # Clear CBCFE (bit 6) in menvcfg
    csrr   t0, menvcfg
    li     t1, ~(1 << 6)          # mask with bit 6 cleared
    and    t0, t0, t1
    csrw   menvcfg, t0

    # Set trap handler address in mtvec
    la     t0, trap_handler
    csrw   mtvec, t0

    # Initialize flag register
    li     t2, 0

    # Switch to supervisor mode (less privileged than M)
    call   machine_to_supervisor

    # In S-mode: attempt CBO.CLEAN, should raise illegal-instruction trap
    mv     a0, zero                # rs1 = x0 (address zero)
    cbo.clean a0

    # Execution resumes here after trap handling
    # t2 should be set to 1 by the trap handler
    j      end_test

trap_handler:
    # Indicate that trap occurred
    li     t2, 1

    # Advance mepc to skip the illegal instruction (4 bytes)
    csrr   t0, mepc
    addi   t0, t0, 4
    csrw   mepc, t0

    mret

end_test:
    nop
# Result: t2 = 1

andi t0, t0, 0x1        # Mask SSE bit (placeholder)
mv a0, t0                # Save menvcfg.SSE result
call machine_to_supervisor
csrr t1, 0x10a          # Read senvcfg
andi t1, t1, 0x1        # Mask SSE bit (placeholder)
mv a1, t1                # Save senvcfg.SSE result
# Result: a0 = 0 & a1 = 0

# Extension Must Be Active: NONE
    la t0, trap_handler
    csrw mtvec, t0
ebreak_inst:
    ebreak
    nop
    j exit
trap_handler:
    csrr t1, mepc
    la t2, ebreak_inst
    li t3, 0
    beq t1, t2, set_one
    j cont
set_one:
    li t3, 1
cont:
    mv a0, t3
    csrr t4, mepc
    addi t4, t4, 4
    csrw mepc, t4
    mret
exit:
# Result: a0 = 1

# Extension Must Be Active: NONE
    csrr a0, 0xb02          # read minstret before ECALL
    auipc t0, 0
    addi t0, t0, trap_handler - .
    csrw mtvec, t0          # set trap vector to trap_handler (direct mode)
    ecall                   # synchronous exception, should not increment minstret
    # execution resumes here after mret in trap handler
    # a2 contains the comparison result (0 if equal)
    nop                     # placeholder to keep code contiguous
trap_handler:
    csrr a1, 0xb02          # read minstret after ECALL
    xor a2, a0, a1          # a2 = 0 if minstret unchanged
    mret
# Result: a2 = 0

# Set MPP field to M (bits 12:11 = 0b11) by writing full mstatus
lui t1, 0x1             # t1 = 0x1000
addi t1, t1, 0x800      # t1 = 0x1800 (MPP=M, other bits cleared)
csrw 0x300, t1         # write mstatus with MPP = M

mret                    # xRET instruction executed in M-mode

csrr a1, 0xb02          # a1 = minstret after mret
# Result: a1 != a0

##########

FIELDS: 63..0=COUNT
MINIMUM PRIVILEGE REQUIRED TO ACCESS THIS REGISTER: M-mode

####################

Note:
+ <result> must be a single register condition.
+ <result> must be in this format: <register> <operator> <value>.
+ <register> should be general-purpose registers (it can be floating-point registers also, or vector registers).
+ <operator> can either be '=' or '!='.
+ <value> can either be hexadecimal or decimal.
+ There can be multiple <result> (for example, # Result: sstatus != 0xffffffff & sstatus != 0xffffffffffffffff) (another example, # Result: a0 = 0 | a1 = 0).

+ <extension> must be NONE if the rule doesn't explicitly say about any extension required.
+ The kernel always starts in M-mode.

# Extension Must Be Active: NONE

li a0, user_code        # a0 = address of user code

user_code:
    li t3, 0x0          # faulting address (null page)

sub t2, t1, t0          # compute difference
# Result: t2 = 1

# Extension Must Be Active: NONE

li t0, 0x8000000000000000          # SATP MODE = Sv39 (8) in bits 63..60

or t0, t0, t1                     # combine MODE and ASID
csrw satp, t0                     # write SATP
csrr t2, satp                     # read back SATP
srli t3, t2, 44                   # shift to obtain ASID field
andi t3, t3, 0xFFFF               # mask to low 16 bits (isolates ASID bits)
# Result: t3 != 0

RESPONSE:
=========

# Extension Must Be Active: NONE
call machine_to_supervisor
# store sentinel value at a known physical address
li t0, 0xdeadbeefdeadbeef
li t1, 0x80001000
sd t0, 0(t1)
# allocate a root page table at 0x80002000 and create a leaf PTE mapping VA 0x400000 -> PA 0x80001000
li t2, 0x80002000
# leaf PTE: (PPN<<10) | V|R|W|X = (0x80001000>>12)<<10 | 0xf
li t3, 0x80001000
srli t3, t3, 12
slli t3, t3, 10
li t4, 0xf
or t3, t3, t4
sd t3, 0(t2)            # store leaf PTE at entry 0
# write satp with MODE=Sv39 (8), ASID=0, PPN = root PT >>12
li t5, 0x80002000
srli t5, t5, 12          # PPN
li t6, 8
slli t6, t6, 60
or t5, t5, t6
csrw satp, t5
# load from virtual address that requires translation (should succeed without SFENCE.VMA)
li t7, 0x400000
ld a0, 0(t7)
# read back satp mode bits for verification
csrr t0, satp
li t1, 0xf
slli t1, t1, 60
and t0, t0, t1
srli t0, t0, 60          # t0 now holds MODE
# Result: a0 = 0xdeadbeefdeadbeef & t0 = 8

sd t3, 0(t0)             # write PTE for VA 0x0
li t4, 8
slli t4, t4, 60          # MODE = Sv39
srli t5, t0, 12
or t4, t4, t5
csrw satp, t4            # activate page table
ld a0, 0x0               # first load, caches translation
li t7, 0x123456789abcdef0
sd t7, 0x3000            # new data at physical 0x3000
li t8, 0x3000 | 0x7       # updated PTE for VA 0x0
sd t8, 0(t0)             # modify page table entry
csrw satp, t4            # second write to satp (no cache invalidation)
ld a1, 0x0               # second load should see old translation
# Result: a1 = 0xdeadbeefcafebabe
====================================================== END - 311 =======================================================

+ <result> must be a single register condition.

+ <register> should be general-purpose registers (it can be floating-point registers also, or vector registers).
+ <operator> can either be '=' or '!='.
+ <value> can either be hexadecimal or decimal.

# Result: a0 = 3

csrrs x0, sstatus, t0      # enable SIE in sstatus
li t1, 0x100               # bit 8 (ECALL from U-mode) in medeleg

# Extension Must Be Active: NONE
call machine_to_supervisor
li t0, 0x40000000        # user‑accessible address (example)
li t1, 0x40000            # 1 << 18  (SUM bit)
csrrc x0, sstatus, t1     # clear SUM => sstatus.SUM = 0
auipc t2, 0               # t2 = PC of this instruction
addi t2, t2, 16           # point to trap_handler (16 bytes ahead)
csrw stvec, t2            # set trap vector to handler
sd t0, 0(t0)               # S‑mode store to user page -> should trap
j done                    # skip over handler after return
trap_handler:
csrr t0, scause           # capture exception cause
sret
done:

+ <result> must be a single register condition.
+ <result> must be in this format: <register> <operator> <value>.
+ <register> should be general-purpose registers (it can be floating-point registers also, or vector registers).
+ <operator> can either be '=' or '!='.
+ <value> can either be hexadecimal or decimal.
+ There can be multiple <result> (for example, # Result: sstatus != 0xffffffff & sstatus != 0xffffffffffffffff) (another example, # Result: a0 = 0 | a1 = 0).
+ The initial values of all general-purpose registers, or floating, or vector registers, are 0.
+ Since the initial values of many CSR fields are unknown and may contain implementation-defined bits, generate test cases that isolate the target field using a bitwise AND mask on the CSR readback value instead of checking the full CSR value directly.

    add t0, t1, t2            # create unaligned address (lower two bits = 11)

    auipc a0, 0                 # a0 = current PC (aligned start of handler)

# Extension Must Be Active: NONE
call machine_to_supervisor
# Set up stvec in vectored mode with a known base address
la t0, vect_base
ori t0, t0, 1            # MODE = 1 (vectored)
csrw stvec, t0

# Enable supervisor timer interrupt
li t1, (1 << 5)          # STIE bit
csrs sie, t1

# Pend a supervisor timer interrupt
csrs sip, t1

# Next instruction will cause the interrupt and jump to the handler
nop

# ------- Padding to reach BASE+20 (5*4) -------
nop                     # offset +4
nop                     # offset +8
nop                     # offset +12
nop                     # offset +16
# ------- Handler entry (BASE + 20) -------
li a0, 0xdeadbeef        # Set a0 to a known value
ebreak                   # Stop execution

vect_base:
    nop                 # Base address (offset 0)

# Result: a0 = 0xdeadbeef

THE RULE:
If an instruction attempts to read the CSR at address 0x00F, the read succeeds only in privilege levels that permit User‑mode CSR access; otherwise the read causes an illegal‑instruction exception.

THE BACKGROUND INFORMATION:
##########
REGISTER: vcsr
CSR ADDRESS: 0x00f

    # Set up pointers

THE RULE:

THE BACKGROUND INFORMATION:

FIELDS: 63..0=VALUE

####################

Note:
+ <result> must be a single register condition.
+ <result> must be in this format: <register> <operator> <value>.
+ <register> should be general-purpose registers (it can be floating-point registers also, or vector registers).
+ <operator> can either be '=' or '!='.
+ <value> can either be hexadecimal or decimal.

RESPONSE:
=========
============================================================
# Extension Must Be Active: V
    li      t0, 2                # set initial vstart = 2
    csrw    vstart, t0
    li      t1, 4                # set vl = 4
    csrw    vl, t1

    # initialize destination vector memory (all 0x1111111111111111)
    li      t2, 0x1111111111111111
    sd      t2, 0(sp)
    sd      t2, 8(sp)
    sd      t2, 16(sp)
    sd      t2, 24(sp)

    # load into v1
    vle64.v v1, (sp)              # v1 = [0x11...,0x11...,0x11...,0x11...]

    # initialize source vector memory (all 0x2222222222222222)
    li      t2, 0x2222222222222222
    sd      t2, 32(sp)
    sd      t2, 40(sp)
    sd      t2, 48(sp)
    sd      t2, 56(sp)

    # load into v2
    vle64.v v2, 32(sp)            # v2 = [0x22...,0x22...,0x22...,0x22...]

    # vector add: only body elements (index >= vstart && index < vl) i.e., indices 2‑3 are affected
    vadd.vv v1, v2, v1

    # store result vector
    vse64.v v1, 64(sp)            # result at 64(sp) .. 96(sp)

    # load results into scalar registers
    ld      a0, 64(sp)           # element 0 (should stay 0x1111...)
    ld      a1, 72(sp)           # element 1 (should stay 0x1111...)
    ld      a2, 80(sp)           # element 2 (should become 0x3333...)
    ld      a3, 88(sp)           # element 3 (should become 0x3333...)

    # build check flag in t0
    li      t0, 0
    li      t4, 0x1111111111111111
    beq     a0, t4, set0
    j       skip0
set0:   ori     t0, t0, 1
skip0:
    beq     a1, t4, set1
    j       skip1
set1:   ori     t0, t0, 2
skip1:
    li      t4, 0x3333333333333333
    beq     a2, t4, set2
    j       skip2
set2:   ori     t0, t0, 4
skip2:
    beq     a3, t4, set3
    j       skip3
set3:   ori     t0, t0, 8
skip3:

# Result: t0 = 0xF
====================================================== END - 446 =======================================================

# Extension Must Be Active: V
csrw 0xc21, zero          # set vtype = 0 (VTA=0, VSEW=8, VLMUL=1)
li   t0, 0xaa
vdup.vx v2, t0            # fill v2 with 0xAA (tail element will be 0xAA)
li   t1, 0x01
vdup.vx v0, t1            # fill v0 with 0x01
li   a0, 1
vsetvli t2, a0, e8,m1     # set VL = 1, SEW = 8-bit
vadd.vv v2, v0, v0        # element 0: 0x01+0x01 = 0x02, tail element unchanged (0xAA)
li   a0, 2
vsetvli t2, a0, e8,m1     # set VL = 2 to store both elements
addi sp, sp, -16
vsb v2, (sp)              # store both bytes to stack
lbu  a1, 1(sp)            # load the tail byte (element 1) into a1
# Result: a1 = 0xaa

##########

MINIMUM PRIVILEGE REQUIRED TO ACCESS THIS REGISTER: U-mode
##########
####################

Note:
+ <result> must be a single register condition.
+ <result> must be in this format: <register> <operator> <value>.
+ <register> should be general-purpose registers (it can be floating-point registers also, or vector registers).
+ <operator> can either be '=' or '!='.
+ <value> can either be hexadecimal or decimal.
+ There can be multiple <result> (for example, # Result: sstatus != 0xffffffff & sstatus != 0xffffffffffffffff) (another example, # Result: a0 = 0 | a1 = 0).

+ <extension> must be NONE if the rule doesn't explicitly say about any extension required.
+ The kernel always starts in M-mode.
